summary.orphanNote: Reached Phase-2 extraction only via code-scan of page files + Footer/Navbar/list components. Orphans here mean: no incoming link from any scanned component. They may still be reachable via email, direct URL, or un-scanned UI (e.g. admin sub-nav).

findings.summary: Inkonsistenter Fördercheck-Pfad: /aktuelles/[slug] linkt auf /foerdercheck, alle anderen Seiten auf /foerder-schnellcheck
findings.evidence: <Link href="/foerdercheck" className=...>Fördercheck starten</Link> — Landing page (app/(public)/page.tsx:41) und Preise-Seite nutzen aber /foerder-schnellcheck als Einstiegspfad. Beide Routen existieren, was aber zu uneinheitlicher UX und SEO-Dopplung führen kann.

findings.summary: AGB- und Datenschutz-Seiten enthalten Demo-Disclaimer in Produktionscode
findings.evidence: Diese AGB sind zu Demonstrationszwecken erstellt. Bitte lassen Sie diese von einem Rechtsanwalt überarbeiten...' wird im Footer gerendert. Identische Notiz in datenschutz/page.tsx:282. Rechtliches Compliance-Risiko bei Live-Betrieb.

findings.summary: Passwort-reset Success-Screen (Link href=/login) kann nicht über href weiter zu Dashboard navigieren, sondern nur zum Login — Nutzer muss erneut credentials eingeben obwohl frisch reset
findings.evidence: Nach erfolgreichem Reset wird nur ein statischer Link zu /login angeboten; keine automatische Session-Erstellung. Das ist sicherheitstechnisch ok, aber UX-Friction.

findings.summary: Registrieren-Seite: nach 'requiresVerification' im legacy-Path Navigation zu /verifizieren ohne email-Query
findings.evidence: else { login(res.token, res.user); ... router.push('/verifizieren') } — verifizieren/page.tsx:31 redirected sofort zu /login wenn kein email in URL oder store ist. Fallback funktioniert nur wenn store-user bereits gesetzt ist. Fragiler Code-Pfad.

findings.summary: DSGVO consent bundling on /foerder-schnellcheck/bericht — single checkbox combines privacy-policy acceptance with marketing-email opt-in
findings.evidence: <Label>Ich akzeptiere die Datenschutzerklärung UND möchte E-Mails von fund24 erhalten</Label> — bundled into z.object({ dsgvo: z.boolean() }). TTDSG §25 / DSGVO Art. 7(2) require separate, specific consent for marketing emails vs. contract-based processing.

findings.summary: /programme/[id] is known-dead (H-P1-01): all outgoing links from /programme land on a 404
findings.evidence: await getProgramm(Number(id)) calls /api/foerderprogramme/{id} which 404s. notFound() fires for every ID, so 'Zurück zu Förderungen' and 'Zum Fördercheck' CTAs never render. ProgrammKarte links to /programme/{id} from /programme — entire detail path is broken.

findings.summary: router.push() called during render (React anti-pattern) on chat, ergebnis, bericht pages
findings.evidence: chat/page.tsx:25-28 and ergebnis/page.tsx:16-19 and bericht/page.tsx:46-49 all call router.push() synchronously in the component body (not in useEffect), then 'return null'. Next.js warns against this; it can cause 'Cannot update Router while rendering' errors and navigation race conditions. /profil/page.tsx correctly uses useEffect — inconsistent.

findings.summary: Native alert() used for success feedback on /berater/[id] Anfrage send — inconsistent with Toaster UX used elsewhere
findings.evidence: alert('Ihre Anfrage wurde erfolgreich gesendet!') — blocking browser dialog instead of the project's shadcn Sonner/Toaster component. Degrades UX and breaks design system consistency.

findings.summary: /foerder-schnellcheck does not read ?programm={id} query param sent from /programme/[id]
findings.evidence: The Fördercheck entry page (FoerderSchnellcheckPage) calls analysiereWebsite(data.url) with zero awareness of searchParams. The 'Zum Fördercheck' CTA from /programme/[id] passes ?programm={id} which is silently dropped — user intent lost. Also compounds with H-P1-01 since /programme/[id] never renders anyway.

findings.summary: No consent/DSGVO gate on website URL submission
findings.evidence: Form submits URL to analysiereWebsite() which scrapes the URL on the server with no user checkbox/consent that the URL will be processed + cached. Only the bericht (email) step has DSGVO. URL-scraping is processing of user-supplied data and should have a notice + checkbox at the entry point.

findings.summary: Inconsistent Select implementations between /berater (shadcn Select) and /programme (native HTML <select>)
findings.evidence: ProgrammListe uses native <select> elements with extensive Tailwind classes while /berater uses @/components/ui/select (shadcn). Visual and a11y behavior diverge across two sibling public pages.

findings.summary: Unauthenticated leak (H-P1-02): /antraege/[id] only protects with a post-hydration router.replace('/login') — the page shell renders with header/metadata before the effect runs. No server-side guard.
findings.evidence: useEffect(() => { if (!token) { router.replace('/login'); } }, [token, router]); — runs after hydration; no Next.js middleware/server redirect

findings.summary: PDF download CTA is a mock: clicking invokes alert('PDF-Download wird in Kürze implementiert'). Button is also `disabled` — user sees no feedback at all in normal state.
findings.evidence: const handleDownloadPDF = () => { alert('PDF-Download wird in Kürze implementiert') } ... <Button onClick={handleDownloadPDF} disabled><Download/>Ergebnisse als PDF</Button>

findings.summary: Partial-failure risk in onboarding expertise: sequential `for (const entry of data.entries) { await addExpertise(entry) }` persists entries 1..N-1 server-side if entry N fails, with no rollback or retry-from-N. Error state shows a generic message.
findings.evidence: for (const entry of data.entries) { await addExpertise(entry); } router.push('/onboarding/dienstleistungen')

findings.summary: Partial-failure risk in onboarding dienstleistungen: identical pattern. Client has no idempotency key / no 'already-added' deduplication — re-submit after partial failure would duplicate entries.
findings.evidence: for (const entry of data.entries) { await addDienstleistung(entry); } router.push('/dashboard/berater')

findings.summary: Inconsistent UX: DokumenteListe uses native browser confirm() for document delete — all other destructive actions in the Antrag detail (revoke ACL) use inline buttons with no confirmation.
findings.evidence: if (!confirm('Dokument wirklich löschen?')) return;

findings.summary: Berater-role onboarding pages (profil/expertise/dienstleistungen) rely only on client-side `istBerater()` checks inside useEffect. Unternehmen users hitting these URLs see the full page before redirect fires.
findings.evidence: useEffect(() => { if (!token) router.replace('/login'); if (nutzer && !istBerater()) router.replace('/dashboard/unternehmen'); }, ...)

findings.summary: Analyse page polls for up to 120s, then shows a fallback 'Zum Dashboard' button but no retry/refresh action — if backend status is slow, user must leave.
findings.evidence: {timeout ? (<Button onClick={() => router.push('/dashboard/unternehmen')}>Zum Dashboard</Button>) : ...}

findings.summary: Dead link to /dashboard/checks/[id] — route does not exist
findings.evidence: Link href=`/dashboard/checks/${check.id}`; glob app/dashboard/checks/** returns no files (confirms H-P1-05).

findings.summary: Silent data loss on Berater-Profil form (H-P1-04)
findings.evidence: Form collects spezialisierungen + websiteUrl (zod schema line 26–27) but updateBeraterProfil payload at lines 93–102 omits them — user edits are silently dropped.

findings.summary: Admin Provisionen uses free-text notiz field instead of structured columns, hardcoded 9,99% rate
findings.evidence: handleBetragChange serializes `bewilligt: ${betrag} EUR` into notiz (line 110); header label shows hardcoded 'Provision (9,99%)' (line 197); provisionBetrag is only updated in local state, never persisted server-side.

findings.summary: Native confirm() dialog used in Vorlagen delete
findings.evidence: `if (!confirm('Vorlage wirklich löschen?')) return;` — blocking native dialog, inconsistent with app-wide UI Dialog.

findings.summary: Native confirm() dialog used in Admin Users deactivate
findings.evidence: `if (!confirm('Diesen Nutzer wirklich deaktivieren?')) return` — bypasses shadcn Dialog / AlertDialog; inconsistent UX.

findings.summary: Dead 'Nachricht' button on unternehmen/anfragen (no onClick)
findings.evidence: <Button> for accepted Anfrage is rendered without any onClick handler; clicking does nothing.

findings.summary: Favoriten delete has no confirmation (destructive action)
findings.evidence: Trash button calls handleRemove(id) immediately — no confirm() or dialog guard for destructive favorite removal.

findings.summary: Berater dashboard labels 'Provisionen' but links to /abwicklung
findings.evidence: Button labelled 'Provisionen' navigates to /dashboard/berater/abwicklung — label/target mismatch.

findings.summary: Berater dashboard renders anfrage.nachricht under 'Dienstleistung:' label
findings.evidence:
Dienstleistung: {anfrage.nachricht || '-'}

— data-model mismatch, `nachricht` is free-text message, not service-id.

findings.summary: Bericht autosave uses onBlur — user losing focus in unusual flows may skip save
findings.evidence: All 5 fields call onBlur={handleBlur}; no debounced interval save. If user closes tab with focused field, changes may not persist (router.replace on create also relies on blur).

findings.summary: Audit-logs useCallback uses `offset` in deps but initial load only depends on `token`
findings.evidence: load callback deps include [filter, offset]; useEffect at line 74 ignores exhaustive-deps — filter changes via typing do not auto-reload (needs Filtern click); acceptable but brittle.
